english: Whose pure lotus feet are served by Indra the king of gods
Who wears a serpent as sacred thread and the moon as crest jewel, the merciful one
Worshipped by yogis like Narada, clad in the sky (sky-clad)
I worship Kaal Bhairava, the supreme lord of Kashi
Resplendent as a crore suns, the supreme liberator from the ocean of existence
The blue-throated one with three eyes who grants desired boons
The destroyer of death, lotus-eyed, bearing the rosary and trident, the imperishable
I worship Kaal Bhairava, the supreme lord of Kashi
Who holds trident, axe, noose and staff in his hands, the primordial cause
The dark-bodied primal deity, imperishable and free from all disease
Of fearsome valour and mastery, fond of the wondrous Tandava dance
I worship Kaal Bhairava, the supreme lord of Kashi
Leader of the hosts of beings, bestower of vast glory
Who purifies the sins and merits of those who dwell in Kashi, the all-pervading
Expert in the paths of righteousness, the ancient Lord of the universe
I worship Kaal Bhairava, the supreme lord of Kashi
